sample_idx = blockIdx.x / num_groups, so max sample_idx = (batch_size*num_groups -1) // num_groups = batch_size - 1 (since integer division). So sample_idx max is batch_size-1. Original condition: sample_idx >= batch_size is always false because sample_idx <= batch_size-1. Mutated condition: sample_idx > batch_size is also always false because sample_idx <= batch_size-1 < batch_size. So both conditions are always false, so the early return never triggers. Therefore the mutation has no effect on the kernel behavior. It is semantically equivalent under all possible inputs (since the condition is always false). Thus the mutant is unkillable.
